title: Workshop Manager (Hands On Technical, Workshop Management)
description:
Job Responsibilities:


Workshop Operations & Technical Oversight

  • Oversee daily workshop operations, ensuring timely, efficient, and high-quality vehicle servicing and repairs

  • Provide technical guidance and support to technicians, including diagnostics, troubleshooting, and complex repairs

  • Ensure all work is carried out in accordance with manufacturer standards, safety regulations, and company procedures

  • Monitor job progress, turnaround time, and productivity to meet service KPI.

Team Leadership & People Management

  • Lead, mentor, and supervise a team of technicians and workshop staff

  • Conduct performance evaluations, coaching, and on-the-job training to enhance technical competency

  • Plan manpower allocation, work scheduling, and leave management

  • Foster a positive, disciplined, and safety-conscious work environment

Customer Service & Quality Control

  • Support service advisors in resolving technical issues and customer concerns

  • Ensure customer complaints are investigated promptly and resolved professionally

  • Implement quality control checks to maintain high service standards and customer satisfaction

Workshop Administration & Cost Control

  • Manage workshop resources including tools, equipment, and consumables

  • Monitor parts usage, wastage, and inventory control in collaboration with parts department

  • Ensure compliance with workplace safety, housekeeping, and regulatory requirements

  • Prepare operational reports on productivity, efficiency, and workshop performance


Job Requirements:

  • Diploma or Technical Certification in Automotive Engineering or related field

  • With 5 – 8 years of automotive workshop experience, preferably in an authorized car dealership environment, and at least 2 – 3 years in a supervisory or managerial role.

  • Strong hands-on technical knowledge in vehicle diagnostics, repair, and maintenance

  • Proven leadership and people management skills

  • Good problem-solving, planning, and organizational abilities

  • Ability to communicate effectively with technicians, service advisors, and management

  • Familiar with workshop management systems and diagnostic tools


HOW TO APPLY:


 

Interested applicants, please click on “Apply Now”. We regret only shortlisted candidates will be notified.


 

Important Note: Trust Recruit Pte Ltd is committed to safeguarding your personal data in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA).


 

Please read our privacy statement on our corporate website www.trustrecruit.com.sg.

Trust Recruit is Singapore’s leading relationship-based human resource and recruitment firm.


With more than 20 years of collective consulting experience, we provide personalized workforce solutions for our corporate clients, ranging from multinational corporations to the public sector, as well as small and medium enterprises.


At Trust Recruit, integrity and reliability permeate every level of our recruitment process.


TRUST is our currency of interactions; be it with our valued clients, talented job-seekers, or our own employees. Through our premium staffing services, we are committed to connecting and developing quality relationships between candidates and companies.
